original_hypothesis.title: Admin Privilege Abuse: Draining Reward Tokens via recover Function
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The recover function in LockingMultiRewards protects the staking token but lacks any protection for reward tokens, allowing the owner to steal all user rewards.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The function explicitly checks if the token is the stakingToken to prevent skimming staked funds, but allows arbitrary withdrawal of any other token, including active reward tokens.

proof_of_signal.pattern: tokenAddress.safeTransfer(owner, tokenAmount) with no check for rewardTokens

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Reward tokens can be drained, causing insolvency
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Check if tokenAddress is in rewardTokens array

analysis_questions: Does the protocol intend for the owner to be able to withdraw active reward tokens?
What happens to users when they try to claim rewards after a drain?
validation_requirements: Call recover on an active reward token
Verify users cannot claim rewards due to insufficient balance

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: A compromised admin calls recover() on the ARB reward token, draining the contract. When users' locks expire, their getRewards() calls revert because the contract lacks the ARB to pay them.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Loss of all pending user rewards

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing test ensuring active reward tokens cannot be recovered

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Large reward token transfers to owner

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
